chunks.title: Introduction to Non-Toxic Kitchen
chunks.summary: The video kicks off with an overview of the host's commitment to maintaining a non-toxic kitchen environment. They express their intention to share their kitchen items, cooking methods, and tips to help viewers create a healthier cooking space.
chunks.transcript: Welcome to my non-toxic kitchen tour. In this video, I'm going to show you guys my entire kitchen. Everything in my kitchen, all of the things that are in my kitchen and that are not in my kitchen. I'm going to show you how intentionally I've chosen cookware, silverware, cutting boards, water to avoid toxins, and be in the most healthy space that I can. I'm going to show you what's in my fridge, what's in my freezer, what's in my wine fridge. What's in there might surprise you. And I'm going to show you what's in my pantry. My hope is that with all these non-toxic kitchen hacks that I've got going on here, you'll be able to implement some of this in your kitchen

chunks.title: Cookware Choices
chunks.summary: The host discusses their cookware preferences, emphasizing the use of enameled cast iron and stainless steel while avoiding non-stick options due to concerns about toxins. They stress the importance of investing in quality cookware that can last for generations.

These are my two favorite pieces of cookware. Enameled cast iron from Lake Rouset and stainless steel cookware from AllClad. You guys might say, "Oh my god, that stuff is so expensive."
